output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 4/4.
Batch 1: Batch 1 executed for T1. Verified `TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS` covers 2, 3, 4, and 5 in `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx`; the `TabsList` still uses `grid w-full`, so a 2-tab effect-layer layout will stretch evenly across the panel width. No writable batch checkpoint file was available, and the repository already matched the requested final state, so there is no net diff to persist.
Batch 2: Batch 2 executed for T2. I diffed the six target files first and confirmed the unstaged edits were revert-only removals of the top-drop-zone behavior, not unrelated new work. `git checkout HEAD -- ...` was blocked by sandbox `.git/index.lock` permissions, so I restored the same `HEAD` content via patches instead. Final verification showed no remaining diff or status entries for any of the six files.
Batch 3: Batch 3 executed for T3, T4, and T5. The indicator path now carries both `isNewTrackTop` and `trackId`, the top drop label overlay is absolutely positioned so it no longer affects row layout, and internal clip drags now pass top-insert intent through to `createTrackAndMoveClip`, which inserts at track index `0` when requested. Verification was code-level for this batch; the TypeScript compile check remains queued for T6.
Batch 4: Batch 4 executed for T6. `npx tsc --noEmit` completed successfully with exit code 0 and no TypeScript errors, so the updated `DropIndicatorPosition` plumbing, `createTrackAndMoveClip` call signatures, and `TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS` lookup usage all type-check cleanly.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified `TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS` contains keys 2, 3, 4, and 5, and `TabsList` still applies `grid w-full` with the selected column class. This means a 2-tab effect-layer clip resolves to `grid-cols-2` and will distribute tabs evenly across the available width. `git show HEAD` matches the same state, so no additional persistent diff was required.

Verified each unstaged diff before restore. The changes were pure reverts of the top-drop-zone work: `coordinate-utils.ts` reintroduced negative row detection removal, `drop-position.ts` removed `isNewTrackTop` plumbing, `useExternalDrop.ts` and `useAssetManagement.ts` removed `insertAtTop` handling, `DropIndicator.tsx` collapsed the descriptive top/bottom label, and `TimelineCanvas.tsx` removed the top-zone label row. After restoring the `HEAD` content, `git diff --name-only -- <6 files>` and `git status --short -- <6 files>` both returned empty output, confirming all six files now match committed `HEAD`.

Verified `DropIndicatorPosition` now declares both `isNewTrackTop?: boolean` and `trackId?: string` in `DropIndicator.tsx`. Verified `toIndicatorPosition()` in `useDragCoordinator.ts` forwards both fields from `DropPosition`. Verified the label generation still reads `position?.isNewTrackTop` and updated the effect dependency list to include that field so the top/bottom label stays correct while dragging.

Verified the top indicator div in `TimelineCanvas.tsx` now uses `absolute top-0 left-0 right-0 z-10` and retains `pointer-events-none`. Verified its parent container remains `className="relative"`, so the label will overlay the timeline instead of participating in normal flow and shifting row hit-test geometry.

Verified `createTrackAndMoveClip` now accepts `insertAtTop?: boolean` in both the hook result type and callback implementation. When `insertAtTop` is true it now calls `addTrack(current.resolvedConfig, kind, 0)`. Verified `useClipDrag.ts` forwards `dropPosition.isNewTrackTop` as the 4th argument at the single-clip cross-track drop site. Also verified `addTrack()` in `editor-utils.ts` already supports indexed insertion via `splice`, so index `0` insertion is valid.

Ran `npx tsc --noEmit` from the repository root and it exited successfully with zero output and exit code 0. No TypeScript mismatches were reported at `DropIndicatorPosition` usage sites, `createTrackAndMoveClip` call sites, or the `TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS` lookup in `ClipPanel.tsx`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed in `ClipPanel.tsx` that `TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS` covers 2/3/4/5. The tab list continues to render as `grid w-full`, so when only 2 tabs are visible the applied `grid-cols-2` class will stretch them evenly across the full panel width. This was verified from code; no browser render was run.

Yes. I reviewed each pre-restore diff and confirmed they were revert-only removals of the top-drop-zone feature, with no unrelated new work mixed in. After restoration, both `git diff --name-only -- <6 files>` and `git status --short -- <6 files>` returned empty output, confirming all six files match committed `HEAD`.

Yes. `DropIndicatorPosition` now declares both `isNewTrackTop` and `trackId`, `toIndicatorPosition()` forwards both fields, and the new-track label path still derives `top` vs `bottom` from `position?.isNewTrackTop`. I also added `position?.isNewTrackTop` to the effect dependencies so that label remains accurate during drag updates.

Yes. The top indicator div now has `pointer-events-none absolute top-0 left-0 right-0 z-10`, and its containing timeline section is already `relative`. That makes it an overlay rather than a normal-flow element, so it will not push track rows down. This was verified from the rendered class/style code, not via browser interaction.

Yes. `createTrackAndMoveClip` now accepts `insertAtTop` and passes `0` to `addTrack()` when true, otherwise `undefined`. `useClipDrag` forwards `dropPosition.isNewTrackTop` as the 4th argument. I also verified `addTrack()` already supports indexed insertion with `splice`, so the top-insert path is valid.
Yes. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed with zero errors. There were no reported type mismatches involving `DropIndicatorPosition`, `createTrackAndMoveClip`, or `TAB_COLUMNS_CLASS` lookup sites.
